recurringPattern: Stacked vertical form with labels above inputs, rounded input fields with placeholder text, a helper line below each field, and a full-width primary submit button at the bottom. Often includes a progress indicator if multi-step.
designHarm: The label-above-input pattern is correct for accessibility, but AI-generates it universally without considering context — inline labels, floating labels, or side labels may be better for short or known-value forms. Every input gets a conspicuous helper text line even when unhelpful ('Enter your name'). The stacked layout wastes vertical space on simple forms.
legitimateExceptions: Complex forms where each field genuinely needs explanation (medical, legal, financial)
Mobile-first forms where vertical stacking is the only viable layout
possibleFixtures: Standard stacked form with labels-above and helper text
Compact form with floating labels and inline validation
Multi-column form that respects field relationships
originalObservations: AI-generated forms always stack vertically with the same field spacing, regardless of form length or complexity.
Helper text appears under every field even when the label is self-explanatory.
Input fields consistently use 8px or 12px border-radius and a subtle inner shadow.
